name: Udyogini Scheme
benefits: Loan up to ₹3,00,000 with subsidy (50% SC/ST, 30% others)
details: Udyogini Scheme helps women become self-employed by providing financial support through banks. It offers subsidy on loans and encourages small business activities like tailoring, food processing, and handicrafts.

name: Shrama Shakthi Scheme
benefits: Loan of ₹50,000 with 4% interest. 50% subsidy if repaid properly.
details: Shrama Shakthi Scheme provides financial support and skill training to minority entrepreneurs in Karnataka. It offers a loan of ₹50,000 at low interest and encourages small business activities. If the loan is repaid on time, 50% is converted into subsidy.

name: Arivu Education Loan Scheme
benefits: Loan from ₹50,000 to ₹5,00,000 per year. 2% service charge after course completion.
steps: Receive application ID and wait for approval
details: Arivu Education Loan Scheme supports minority students in Karnataka by providing financial assistance for professional courses like engineering, medical, and management.

name: Bhagya Lakshmi Scheme
benefits: Financial assistance up to ₹34,751 at age 18, annual scholarship (₹300–₹1,000), and health insurance up to ₹25,000 per year
steps: Download application form from official website
Submit to Anganwadi or local office
details: Bhagya Lakshmi Scheme supports girl children in Karnataka by providing financial assistance, scholarships, and insurance benefits to families below the poverty line.

name: Vidyasiri Scheme
benefits: Free hostel, food, and fee assistance
details: Provides hostel, food, and fee support to backward class students pursuing higher education.

name: Post Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
benefits: Tuition fees + maintenance allowance
details: Supports students after Class 10 to continue education in colleges and professional courses.

name: Pre Matric Scholarship (Karnataka)
benefits: Financial support for school education
details: Encourages school education among economically weaker and minority students.

name: Medical Assistance (Karmika Arogya Bhagya)
benefits: ₹300 per day up to ₹20,000 for hospitalization (minimum 48 hours required)
details: This scheme provides financial assistance to registered construction workers and their dependents in Karnataka during hospitalization, ensuring support for medical expenses.

name: Griha Lakshmi Scheme
benefits: ₹2,000 per month financial assistance directly transferred to bank account (DBT)
steps: Register using Aadhaar and mobile number
Submit application and get reference number
details: Griha Lakshmi Scheme provides monthly financial assistance to women heads of households in Karnataka to support household expenses and improve financial stability.

schemes.scheme_name: Compensation for Loss of Life and Property Caused by Wild Animals in and around Forest Areas
schemes.sub_scheme: Assistance for Damage to Houses Due to Wild Elephant Attacks
schemes.details: Financial assistance to residents whose houses, huts, or boundary walls are damaged by wild elephants.
eligibility: Applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand
Damage must be caused by wild elephants in forest areas

steps: Report incident to Range Office within 2 days
Verification by Forest Inspector or Deputy Forest Range Officer
Joint assessment by Naib Tehsildar and Range Officer
Final investigation by Assistant Conservator of Forests
Approval and compensation by Divisional Forest Officer

schemes.department: Labour Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh
schemes.details: Night shelters for construction workers and their families who temporarily relocate for work.
benefits: Night shelters for construction workers
Accommodation for family members

registration: Worked at least 90 days in last 12 months
application: Construction worker with valid identity card

steps: Visit Municipal Corporation or Nagar Palika office
Fill mandatory details and attach documents

schemes.scheme_name: Construction of Free Houses for Tribals
schemes.department: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department
schemes.details: Free house construction for tribal communities who have house site pattas.
benefits: Free house construction
Improved living conditions with permanent housing
eligibility: Resident of Tamil Nadu
Belongs to SC/ST community
Must possess free house site patta

steps: Collect application form from Tribal Welfare office

schemes.scheme_name: Construction of Hostels for OBC Boys and Girls
schemes.department: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
schemes.details: Financial assistance for construction of hostels to support OBC students from rural and poor backgrounds.

funding_pattern.boys_hostel: 60 percent central government, 40 percent state government
funding_pattern.girls_hostel: 90 percent central government, 10 percent state government

students: 5 percent seats reserved for students with disabilities

application_process.submission_address: Joint Secretary (Backward Classes), Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Shastri Bhavan, New Delhi

documents_required: Financial statements of last two years

schemes.department: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department
schemes.details: Affordable hostel accommodation for working women living away from families.
eligibility: Applicant must be a working woman
Income should not exceed 25000 in Chennai
Income should not exceed 15000 in other districts

scheme_name: Construction of Godown for Grain Storage
details: The scheme aims to provide modern and scientific food grain storage facilities in rural areas under the Food Security Act. Applicants must apply through the concerned Gram Panchayat.
benefits: Construction of modern and scientific food grain storage facilities
eligibility: Applicant should be located in a rural area of Madhya Pradesh
Applicant should be engaged in agriculture or grain storage related activities
Applicant should have access to at least one acre of land or land allotted by the Cooperative Department

steps: Visit the official Panchayat Darpan 2.0 website
Fill all mandatory fields and upload required documents
Submit the application and note the Application Number

post_application_process: If the application is correct, Gram Panchayat issues notice for building permission fee
Applicant must deposit the prescribed fee before the due date
After fee payment, construction permission will be issued

schemes.scheme_name: Coaching Help Scheme for JEE-GUJCET-NEET Exams
schemes.implemented_by: Gujarat Unreserved Education and Economical Development Corporation

benefits.financial_assistance: ₹20,000 per year per student or actual coaching fee whichever is lower

eligibility: Applicant must belong to non-reserved category
Applicant must have 70% or more marks in Class 10/11/12
Applicant must be from Science stream
Applicant must be preparing for JEE, GUJCET or NEET
Coaching institute must have at least 3 years of experience
Annual family income must be ₹4,50,000 or less

steps: Visit official website of Gujarat Unreserved Education and Economical Development Corporation
Click on Coaching Help Scheme for JEE-GUJCET-NEET
Login and fill the application form
Submit application and note application number
Take printout and send signed copy with documents to Deputy Director or District Social Welfare Officer

documents_required: Proof of coaching institute registration (3 years)

additional: Option to withdraw amount after scheme period
Option to convert amount into pension scheme

eligibility: Applicant must be an Indian citizen
Applicant must be at least 18 years old
Applicant must be a marginal worker
Annual income should not exceed ₹48,000
Applicant must be a permanent resident of Jammu and Kashmir
Applicant or spouse should not be beneficiary of another scheme

steps: Visit Tehsil or District Social Welfare Office
Submit to District Social Welfare Officer

documents_required: Residence proof of Jammu and Kashmir

schemes.department: Karnataka Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
eligibility: Applicant must be registered construction worker
Must have paid subscription for at least 3 continuous years
Must remain active construction worker
Age must be below 60 years
Should not receive similar pension from other schemes

steps: Register as construction worker on KBOCWWB website
Fill application form and upload documents

documents_required: Employment certificate or 90 days work certificate

schemes.scheme_name: Cost of Hearing Aid for Construction Workers
schemes.department: Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

benefits.financial_assistance: Up to ₹5,000 for hearing aid purchase

eligibility: Applicant must be resident of West Bengal
Applicant must be a construction worker
Age between 18 and 60 years
Must have worked at least 90 days in a year
Must be registered member of BOCW Welfare Board

steps: Request Form-27 from Assistant Labour Commissioner

benefits.financial_assistance: 90% of actual cost or ₹4,000 whichever is less

eligibility: Applicant must be registered construction worker
Must have valid labour identity card
Must have worked as construction worker for 3 consecutive years
Cycle purchased under scheme cannot be sold for 3 years

steps: Rural area: Chief Executive Officer, Janpad Panchayat
Urban area: Municipal Corporation or Municipality office
Apply within 3 months from purchase of cycle

benefits: Training in Heavy Vehicle Driving
Training in Short Hand
Security Services Training
Office Soft Skill Training
Retail Sales Training
Beauty Parlor Course for Male and Female
eligibility: Applicant must belong to minority community
Applicant must be a permanent resident of Karnataka
Age must be between 18 and 35 years
Annual family income less than ₹1,03,000 in urban areas
Annual family income less than ₹96,000 in rural areas
Minimum qualification SSLC
Applicant must not have taken training under other government schemes

steps: Visit Karnataka Minorities Development Corporation website
Click Apply Online and enter mobile number
Enter Aadhaar number and verify OTP
Submit application and receive application ID

benefits: 1.5 year diploma courses in Food Processing
Bakery and Confectionery training
Cookery training
eligibility: Applicant must be resident of Uttarakhand
Intermediate (Science/Agriculture) for Food Processing course
Intermediate qualification for other courses

steps: Visit nearest Food Department Training Centre
Selection based on Intermediate marks merit

documents_required: Documents required as per training centre guidelines

schemes.scheme_name: Group Supply Of Goats And Sheep
benefits: 10 goats and 1 buck provided on 50% subsidy
Improves income source for tribal farmers
Supports employment in tribal areas
eligibility: Beneficiary must belong to Scheduled Tribe
Beneficiary must be below poverty line

steps: Apply through Panchayat Samiti or Zilla Parishad

benefits.superannuation_pension: Available after retirement at 58 years with 10 years service
benefits.early_pension: Available between age 50 and 58 with reduction

eligibility: Applicant must be member of EPF scheme
Monthly pay must not exceed ₹15000
Minimum 10 years service required
Must have contributed to Employees Pension Fund

benefits.unemployment_allowance: ₹10000 per month while waiting for deployment

eligibility: Applicant must be domicile of Haryana
Age between 18 and 35 years
Must be unemployed
Graduate or postgraduate in IT related field
Annual family income must not exceed ₹3 lakh for unemployment allowance

steps: Visit official IT Saksham Yuva website
